# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from 'http';  //Import required types to use Next.js's http library  
const next = require('next');             // Import the main middleware for express applications (needed)     
// Initialize your own app in order not affect other instances of it:     .init()    if this file is called as a serverless function,  else using async normal node or deno.js environment setup next().getRequest(req).setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', 'true')  
const app = await (next({ type: 'server' })) //initialize your Next application for the request/response objects    if(!app) throw new Error ('Could not initialize Express / Next.js application');  .getResponse() in case next middleware is called as a serverless function else use http or https packages to get response from express: app  
const handler = (req, res): void => { //handler for req/res objects which will be provided by Vercel and run the Next.js request-response lifecycle     .send(html);    }  }) if (!app || !serverlessFunction) throw new Error('Could not mount Express / Next App to path'); app['vercel'].ready().then(() => { server = next({ type: 'server', req, res }); const handleRequest = (req, res): void | null => 
{ try  //try block for handling errors   if (!res.finished) return;    this is where your main logic will be written and it depends on what you want to do in a successful or error case like logging out the user using JWT based token storage/retrieval, redirecting them with appropriate status code etc...
  { // If we get here then all of our checks have passed successfully    return server.getResponse()(req, res); } catch (error) {{ console .log('An error occurred', e));   if (!res.finished){return next({ type: 'httpError' });}// set http status code and end the request
  { //If we got this far then it means an unknown serverless function failed or a new Error was thrown, log that to console } return; }} run your middleware with express req / res objects   app['vercel'].ready().then(() => handler(req as IncomingMessage & ServerResponse | (IncomingRequestCarbon)=> void)); //This calls the Express.js handlers and logs errors if they occur, allowing Vercel to capture all requests 
})}; handleCookies = { name: 'auth', maxAge:  604800 };    return app['vercel'].ready().then(() => next({ type:'httpError' })).catch((err)=>{console.log('An error occurred during startup ', err); process .exit(1)} )
